definition xtea_64_128_round_function :: "32 word \<Rightarrow> 32 word" where
  "xtea_64_128_round_function x = xor (push_bit 4 x) (drop_bit 5 x) + x"

definition xtea_64_128_encrypt_half_round ::
  "32 word \<Rightarrow> 32 word \<Rightarrow> 32 word \<Rightarrow> 32 word \<Rightarrow> 32 word" where
  "xtea_64_128_encrypt_half_round updating source key_word sum_a =
     updating + xor (xtea_64_128_round_function source) (sum_a + key_word)"

definition xtea_64_128_select_key_word :: "32 word list \<Rightarrow> 32 word \<Rightarrow> nat \<Rightarrow> 32 word" where
  "xtea_64_128_select_key_word key_words sum_a shift =
     key_words ! unat (and (drop_bit shift sum_a) 3)"

definition xtea_64_128_key_to_words :: "128 word \<Rightarrow> 32 word list" where
  "xtea_64_128_key_to_words key =
     map (\<lambda>i. ucast (drop_bit (32 * (3 - i)) key) :: 32 word) [0, 1, 2, 3]"

instruction:
Translate the XTEA-64/128 Encrypt Cycle (encrypt_cycle) from Python to Isabelle/HOL.
This is a T4 Top-Level Orchestration component.
Apply these transformations: Convert iterative loops to recursive functions..
Preserve the semantics of `encrypt_cycle`.
input:
def xtea_64_128_encrypt_cycle(v0: int, v1: int, sum_: int, key_words: list[int]) -> tuple[int, int, int]:
    """One full XTEA cycle: update v0, advance sum, update v1."""
    k0 = xtea_64_128_select_key_word(key_words, sum_, 0)
    v0 = xtea_64_128_encrypt_half_round(v0, v1, k0, sum_)
    sum_ = (sum_ + DELTA) & WORD_MASK
    k1 = xtea_64_128_select_key_word(key_words, sum_, 11)
    v1 = xtea_64_128_encrypt_half_round(v1, v0, k1, sum_)
    return v0, v1, sum_

semantics.first_line: def xtea_64_128_encrypt_cycle(v0: int, v1: int, sum_: int, key_words: list[int]) -> tuple[int, int, int]:

instruction:
Translate the XTEA-64/128 Decrypt Cycle (decrypt_cycle) from Python to Isabelle/HOL.
This is a T4 Top-Level Orchestration component.
Apply these transformations: Convert iterative loops to recursive functions..
Preserve the semantics of `decrypt_cycle`.
input:
def xtea_64_128_decrypt_cycle(v0: int, v1: int, sum_: int, key_words: list[int]) -> tuple[int, int, int]:
    """Inverse of one full XTEA cycle."""
    k1 = xtea_64_128_select_key_word(key_words, sum_, 11)
    v1 = xtea_64_128_decrypt_half_round(v1, v0, k1, sum_)
    sum_ = (sum_ - DELTA) & WORD_MASK
    k0 = xtea_64_128_select_key_word(key_words, sum_, 0)
    v0 = xtea_64_128_decrypt_half_round(v0, v1, k0, sum_)
    return v0, v1, sum_

semantics.first_line: def xtea_64_128_decrypt_cycle(v0: int, v1: int, sum_: int, key_words: list[int]) -> tuple[int, int, int]:

instruction:
Translate the XTEA-64/128 Encrypt Round Iterator (encrypt_rounds_iterate) from Python to Isabelle/HOL.
This is a T4 Top-Level Orchestration component.
Apply these transformations: Use Isabelle word library operations.; Convert iterative loops to recursive functions..
Preserve the semantics of `encrypt_rounds_iterate`.
output:
function xtea_64_128_encrypt_rounds_iterate ::
  "32 word \<Rightarrow> 32 word \<Rightarrow> 32 word \<Rightarrow> 32 word list \<Rightarrow> nat \<Rightarrow> (32 word \<times> 32 word)" where
  "xtea_64_128_encrypt_rounds_iterate v0 v1 sum_a key_words i =
     (if i \<ge> xtea_64_128_rounds then (v0, v1)
      else
        let (v0a, v1a, sum_b) = xtea_64_128_encrypt_cycle v0 v1 sum_a key_words
        in xtea_64_128_encrypt_rounds_iterate v0a v1a sum_b key_words (i + 1))"
  by pat_completeness auto

termination xtea_64_128_encrypt_rounds_iterate
  by (relation "measure (\<lambda>(v0, v1, sum_a, key_words, i). xtea_64_128_rounds - i)")
     (auto simp: xtea_64_128_rounds_def)

      else

     (auto simp: xtea_64_128_rounds_def)

instruction:
Translate the XTEA-64/128 Top-Level Decrypt (decrypt) from Python to Isabelle/HOL.
This is a T4 Top-Level Orchestration component.
Preserve the semantics of `decrypt`.
input:
def xtea_64_128_decrypt(ciphertext: int, master_key: int) -> int:
    """Decrypt one 64-bit block under one 128-bit key."""
    key_words = xtea_64_128_key_to_words(master_key)
    initial_sum = (DELTA * ROUNDS) & WORD_MASK
    v0, v1 = xtea_64_128_block_to_words(ciphertext & BLOCK_MASK)
    v0, v1 = xtea_64_128_decrypt_rounds_iterate(v0, v1, initial_sum, key_words, 0)
    return xtea_64_128_words_to_block(v0, v1)

semantics.first_line: def xtea_64_128_decrypt(ciphertext: int, master_key: int) -> int:
